Fast Company Executive BoardFuture-proof your company with a cultural analysisThe benefits of doing a cultural analyzation allow for planned, future-led strategies that can improve your internal communications, help identify areas of process opportunity, establish what your culture should look like today and tomorrow, and make work more meaningful for everyone.

Fast Company Executive BoardIs creativity a gift or a skill? Thinking of new ideas can actually be practicedOnce you have a mountain of ideas, you may find that then, out of nowhere, lightning can strike.
NewsWorking from home damages our eyes. Here’s how you can protect your visionIn a recent survey, 68% of remote workers said they’ve experienced new eye problems since working from home.

Co.DesignDesigning effective disability housing is about far more than ramps and doorwaysSuccessful housing projects share common architectural features: inviting communal spaces, private individual dwellings, commercial opportunities for residents, and on-site support.
Work LifePOV: 5 steps to achieve better sales and marketing alignmentBusinesses are 67% better at closing deals when sales and marketing work together.
Co.DesignAmerican roads are designed to be deadlyStreets have seen record numbers of pedestrian deaths, but design solutions exist.
Work LifeThese are the 4 boundaries your brain needs to feel less overwhelmedSo many of us feel overwhelmed. Setting boundaries allows you work in a more sustainable—and less reactive—way.

ImpactThis new apartment building in Amsterdam is new housing for wildlife, not just humansWith areas specifically designed to be homes for birds, bats, hedgehogs, and fish, the development is designed to increase the city’s biodiversity.

Co.DesignThis sleek beauty line brings the magic of French drugstore brands to the U.S.Garance Doré, one of the original style bloggers, has launched a simple, pared-down line of three classic products.

NewsCOVID-19 boosters for omicron are coming this fall. That may be too late to fight the BA.5 variantRegulators’ slow-moving response to newly emerging coronavirus strains could hinder our ability to fight variants that evade previous immunity.

VideoHow Blueland is expanding beyond cleaning products to personal careBlueland’s fight against single-use plastics has entered the bathroom. Their recently-launched powder to gel body wash uses a “Forever bottle,” furthering their goal to revolutionize the way we clean.

VideoHow to live in a small space, according to IKEAFast Company took a trip to IKEA’s headquarters in Almhult, Sweden, to explore its secret to innovation. IKEA shared how they solved a design issue with flatpack furniture

VideoWhy Unilever blocked the Ben & Jerry's Israeli boycottBen & Jerry’s filed a lawsuit against its parent company, Unilever, to block the sale of its Israeli business. In July 2021, Ben & Jerry's announced that the brand would no longer sell its products in the occupied Palestinian territories, saying it was “inconsistent with our values.” This is Fast Company's Brand hit or miss of the week.

VideoThis startup wants to make rooftop farming the future of sustainabilityGotham Greens, the Brooklyn-based urban-farming startup, aims to prove that growing crops in greenhouses on city rooftops can be more efficient and sustainable than traditional methods.
